 {"id":"bd-3w08","title":"PiWasm: Impl core polyfill (Module/Instance/Memory)","description":"# Goal
Implement the core WebAssembly polyfill surface in PiJS backed by wasmtime.

# Scope
- WebAssembly.instantiate(bytes, imports)
- WebAssembly.Memory (initial/maximum, grow, .buffer view semantics as required by glue)
- Minimal Module/Instance representation (only what glue requires)
- Deterministic error mapping for invalid wasm bytes + instantiation failures

# Constraints
- Generic only (no extension-id branching).
- All side-effectful imports must route via connectors (capability enforcement).

# Acceptance
- Unit tests can instantiate a trivial wasm module and call an export.
- Memory growth boundaries enforced (limits from policy/budgets).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:12:25.923613573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T07:02:05.840567354Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T07:02:03.687991071Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3w08","depends_on_id":"bd-11cz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:12:32.791487011Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3w08","depends_on_id":"bd-1ry","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:12:25.930323990Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":739,"issue_id":"bd-3w08","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Deferred with bd-1ry: no WASM-using extensions exist in the corpus.","created_at":"2026-02-07T07:02:05Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-48tv","title":"Implement LabRuntime deterministic testing for extensions","description":"Use asupersync LabRuntime for deterministic extension testing.

## Background
LabRuntime provides:
- Deterministic scheduling (same input = same output)
- Virtual time (no wall-clock delays)
- Reproducible test runs

## Current State
- Extensions use real runtime for tests
- Timing-sensitive tests may be flaky
- No way to test scheduler determinism

## Implementation Requirements
1. Add test infrastructure using LabRuntime
2. Wrap extension scheduler tests
3. Verify event loop ordering is deterministic
4. Test timeout/cancellation behavior

## Benefits
- Tests run faster (no real delays)
- Tests are reproducible
- Can test edge cases (timeouts, races)

## Test Plan
- Existing scheduler tests pass under LabRuntime
- No timing flakiness in CI

## Files to Modify
- tests/event_loop_conformance.rs (use LabRuntime)
- Add lab runtime setup helper","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:12:38.815290493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:21:45.897971634Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T07:21:45.897901964Z","close_reason":"10 LabRuntime deterministic tests implemented in tests/event_loop_conformance.rs. LabBridgeClock adapter + lab_for_extensions helper. All quality gates pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-48tv","depends_on_id":"bd-20lm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:12:38.837934359Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-48tv","depends_on_id":"bd-2vie","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:13:46.366536955Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":304,"issue_id":"bd-48tv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation complete: 10 LabRuntime-based deterministic tests added to tests/event_loop_conformance.rs. Tests cover: scheduler determinism (same seed → same trace), seed divergence, LabRuntime invariant verification, timer cancellation, virtual time progression, PiEventLoop determinism, hostcall error outcomes, mixed interleaving, and time_until_next_timer accuracy. All 12 tests (2 existing + 10 new) pass. Infrastructure includes LabBridgeClock adapter and lab_for_extensions() helper.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:21:29Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-vs72","title":"Implement pi.model control APIs (setModel, getThinkingLevel, setThinkingLevel)","description":"Allow extensions to control model and thinking level.

## API Spec (from legacy)
```typescript
await pi.setModel(model: Model): Promise<boolean>
pi.getThinkingLevel(): ThinkingLevel  // 'off'|'minimal'|'low'|'medium'|'high'|'xhigh'
pi.setThinkingLevel(level: ThinkingLevel): void
```

## Use Cases
- Auto-switching models based on task complexity
- Preset systems (different models for different workflows)
- Dynamic thinking level adjustment

## Implementation Requirements
1. Hostcall handlers for all three APIs
2. setModel is async (may need provider initialization)
3. Thinking level clamped to model capabilities
4. Model change creates ModelChangeEntry in session

## Behavior Notes
- setModel returns false if model not available
- setThinkingLevel clamped to model's max thinking
- Changes affect next agent turn

## Test Plan
- Unit test: setModel changes current model
- Unit test: getThinkingLevel returns current level
- Unit test: setThinkingLevel clamped correctly
- Integration test: model change persisted to session

## Files to Modify
- src/extensions.rs (hostcall handlers)
- src/agent.rs (model/thinking management)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:11:31.602941272Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:40:07.991999788Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T04:40:07.991936610Z","close_reason":"All four model control APIs (getModel, setModel, getThinkingLevel, setThinkingLevel) are fully implemented: JS bridge functions in extensions_js.rs (lines 2929-2946), pi object exports (lines 3464-3467), Rust hostcall handlers in extensions.rs (lines 5828-5901) with session persistence via ExtensionSession trait, and ExtensionManager backing state.
